title: Component and End-to-End RAG Evaluation · Program Brief: Rag Retrieval Context Evaluation
summary: A team reports one LLM-judge score on generated answers, never measures retrieval, changes the evaluator prompt between runs, and has no human or safety review. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the retrieval context evaluation increment in layered RAG evaluation harness. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Measure candidate recall, ranking, context precision and recall, source diversity, conflict coverage, and citation-map integrity.
Evaluate ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citations, safety, system quality, and user outcome separately and together.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-retrieval-context-evaluation — Measure candidate recall, ranking, context precision and recall, source diversity, conflict coverage, and citation-map integrity.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-end-to-end-evaluation-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-end-to-end-evaluation-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_end_to_end_evaluation_4(retrieval_score: float = 0.82, faithfulness: float = 0.91, answer_relevance: float = 0.84, citation_precision: float = 0.95, safety_pass: bool = True, human_reviewed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    scores = (retrieval_score, faithfulness, answer_relevance, citation_precision)
    if any(score < 0.0 or score > 1.0 for score in scores):
        return False, 0.0, "metric-range"
    if min(scores) < 0.75:
        return False, min(scores), "weak-stage"
    if not safety_pass:
        return False, min(scores), "safety-failure"
    if not human_reviewed:
        return False, min(scores), "judge-only"
    aggregate = sum(scores) / len(scores)
    assert aggregate <= 1.0
    return True, round(aggregate, 4), "layered-evaluation"

# Evidence: rag-retrieval-context-evaluation-zf7vi8

def evidence_rag_retrieval_context_evaluation_zf7vi8(retrieval_score: float = 0.82, faithfulness: float = 0.91, answer_relevance: float = 0.84, citation_precision: float = 0.95, safety_pass: bool = True, human_reviewed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

        "query8iv7fz",
        "tenant2189",
        "indexzf7vi82189",
        "prompt8iv7fz2189",

    assert len(set(evidence_axes_zf7vi8)) == 6

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Retrieval Context Evaluation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6594 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to evaluate retrieval, grounding, answer utility, citations, safety, and human evidence separately; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure candidate recall, ranking, context precision and recall, source diversity, conflict coverage, and citation-map integrity.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Retrieval Context Evaluation model · Theory Rag Retrieval Context Eva….
steps.why: Retrieving Rag Retrieval Context Evaluation through the concrete layered RAG evaluation harness case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Retrieval Context Evaluation model · Theory Rag Retrieval Context Eva…, keep Rag Retrieval Context Evaluation connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure candidate recall, ranking, context precision and recall, source diversity, conflict coverage, and citation-map integrity.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Retrieval Context Evaluation model · Theory Rag Retrieval Context Eva…, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Retrieval Context Evaluation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Retrieval Context Evaluation model · Theory Rag Retrieval Context Eva…, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6594 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to evaluate retrieval, grounding, answer utility, citations, safety, and human evidence separately; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure candidate recall, ranking, context precision and recall, source diversity, conflict coverage, and citation-map integrity.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Retrieval Context Evaluation model · Theory Rag Retrieval Context Eva…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: Context precision alone proves the needed evidence was retrieved.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: rag-retrieval-context-evaluation-zf7vi8(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_rag_retrieval_context_evaluation_zf7vi8\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?any\(score < 0.0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(scores\) < 0.75)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not safety_pass)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not human_reviewed)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
